name: Artifact helpers for initialize, record, and supervisor status
acceptance_tests: Helper scripts can initialize state and results artifacts in a temporary repo
Recording an iteration updates the TSV log and state counters correctly
Supervisor status returns deterministic stop or relaunch decisions
notes: The Python helper layer manages state, TSV logging, and deterministic supervisor decisions.

name: Interactive setup wizard with repo-aware defaults
acceptance_tests: Wizard infers a pytest-based verify command when the repo exposes tests
Wizard offers a default guard when autoresearch supervisor tooling exists
Wizard reports missing required fields before launch
notes: Added scripts/autoresearch_wizard.py plus shared helper logic for setup summaries, missing fields, and clarification questions.

name: Background launch, stop, status, and resume control
acceptance_tests: Background launch writes a launch manifest
Stop requests are persisted in state
Resume clears stop_requested and marks the run active again
notes: The runtime controller now matches the skill contract by supporting resume as well as launch, status, and stop.

name: Specialized workflow references for plan, debug, fix, learn, predict, scenario, security, and ship
acceptance_tests: Skill entrypoint references the workflow documents consistently
README documents the expanded workflow surface
Launcher metadata advertises the expanded routing behavior
notes: Adapted the narrower Codex bundle to support a Claude-autoresearch-style workflow pack without adopting the plugin architecture.

name: Plugin sync workflow and CI enforcement for GitHub-backed installs
acceptance_tests: A maintainer can run a single sync command to mirror SKILL.md, agents, scripts, and references into the packaged plugin payload
GitHub Actions fails when the packaged plugin payload drifts from the root source of truth
Contributor documentation defines the edit, sync, validate, and push workflow for updates that should reach GitHub-backed plugin installs
notes: Added scripts/sync_plugin_payload.py, a contributor-gate GitHub Actions workflow, and packaging docs that explain how pushed changes reach GitHub-backed plugin installs.

name: Local plugin fallback bootstrap for machine-local marketplace
acceptance_tests: Helper script can sync the plugin payload, copy it to a local plugins directory, and merge a local-source entry into a machine-local marketplace manifest
Bootstrapped marketplace identifier correctly matches the enabled-plugin reference
Existing local marketplace entries for unrelated plugins are preserved during bootstrap
notes: Added scripts/bootstrap_local_plugin.py and docs/LOCAL-FALLBACK-BOOTSTRAP.md to handle machine-local Codex plugin discovery when GitHub sync fails or the local runtime rejects non-local marketplace sources.

name: Short runtime re-anchoring contract for resumed and long-running work
acceptance_tests: SKILL.md references the runtime hard invariants document
The runtime hard invariants document gives a short checklist for start, resume, and drift recovery
A focused contract test fails if the runtime hard invariants wiring disappears
notes: Added references/runtime-hard-invariants.md and a lightweight skill-contract test so the compact runtime checklist stays part of the public protocol.

name: Cross-platform contributor gate for packaging and skill validation
acceptance_tests: Packaging mode runs sync, distribution validation, and plugin-distribution tests
Skill mode runs the full pytest suite in addition to packaging checks
The runtime smoke path exercises background launch, status, stop, resume, and complete against a temporary repo
notes: Added scripts/run_contributor_gate.py, focused tests for its planning/runtime-smoke behavior, and documentation/CI wiring so contributors have one obvious validation entrypoint.

name: Subagent-first skill contract and contributor expectations
acceptance_tests: tests/test_skill_contract.py verifies the runtime hard invariants anchor and the contributor-maintenance expectations
CONTRIBUTING.md explains how to update the subagent-first skill contract and the lightweight verification surface together
feature-list.json records the contract work as a tracked feature entry
notes: Tracks the new maintenance contract so future updates keep the verification and contributor guidance aligned.

name: Subagent orchestration helper, continuation policy, and orchestrator-owned state contract
acceptance_tests: scripts/autoresearch_subagent_plan.py emits a stable standing-pool plan with orchestrator ownership and serial fallback metadata
Wizard, state, and launch-manifest payloads include subagent_pool and continuation_policy fields
Hook and contract tests verify the post-launch continuation wording and the orchestrator-only state/result ownership rules
notes: Adds a concrete subagent-pool planner plus helper/runtime metadata so the skill can stay subagent-first without relying on prose alone.

name: Generic start/stop hook runtime with hybrid next-step continuation
acceptance_tests: Installer migrates the managed hook package to ~/.codex/hooks-runtime and rewrites hooks.json to the new start/stop script names
Stop hook continues on supervisor relaunch and tailors the block prompt from the latest task_complete Next step/Next steps section
State-driven stop and needs_human decisions still terminate even when the final response includes Next step text
Archive-ready responses are blocked until the repo is clean, pushed, and PR'd when working on a non-default branch
notes: Renamed the managed hook modules to generic start/stop/common/context names, kept the state supervisor as the hard stop authority, added transcript-based Next step continuation prompts, and added archive-readiness git/publication guards.

name: Public release surface cleanup for handoff and host-specific artifacts
acceptance_tests: Packaging validation passes after removing tracked handoff notes from the repo
Release-oriented tests fail if README reintroduces host-specific scaffold commands or the handoff ignore rule disappears
Contributor docs explain that private handoff notes should stay in ignored local files
notes: Removes the stale Codex handoff document from version control, ignores future HANDOFF files by default, and narrows the published docs to release-relevant instructions.
